---
title: "My Research Report"
author: "Your Name"
format: 
    html:
        self-contained: true
        mathjax: false
editor: visual
jupyter: python3
execute:
    daemon: 60
---

## Introduction

This is a Quarto document. I can write standard Markdown here.

## Data Analysis

Below is a Python cell with a caption and a label for cross-referencing.

```{python}
#| eval: false
#| label: fig-polar
#| fig-cap: "A line plot on a polar axis"

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

r = np.arange(0, 2, 0.01)
theta = 2 * np.pi * r
fig, ax = plt.subplots(subplot_kw={'projection': 'polar'})
ax.plot(theta, r)
ax.set_rticks([0.5, 1, 1.5, 2])
ax.grid(True)
plt.show()


#| label: fig-growth
#| fig-cap: "Economic growth projections for 2026."

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.plot([1, 2, 3], [10, 20, 30])
plt.show()

```

Notice in @fig-growth, we have an issue where it is too linear for us.
